title: malva plazzae
intro: Malva plazzae, also known as marshmallow, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant contains mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Malva plazzae has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Malva plazzae include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to calm irritated skin, soothe digestive issues, and aid in the relief of respiratory problems.
